sections.title: STANDARD FOR TANNIA1
sections.text:
Adopted in 2001. Amended in 2005, 2011.

1  Commonly known in certain regions by: tania, yautia, new cocoyam, tanier, chou Caraïbes, taioba, mangareto, mangarito, mangarás, yautía, malanga, macal, quiscamote, tiquisque, otó, okumo, uncucha, gualuza, malangay, queiquexque, taniera, macabo, etc.

page_spans.text:
Adopted in 2001. Amended in 2005, 2011.

1  Commonly known in certain regions by: tania, yautia, new cocoyam, tanier, chou Caraïbes, taioba, mangareto, mangarito, mangarás, yautía, malanga, macal, quiscamote, tiquisque, otó, okumo, uncucha, gualuza, malangay, queiquexque, taniera, macabo, etc.

sections.title: DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
sections.text: This Standard applies to the tubercles of commercial varieties of lilac tannia grown from Xanthosoma violaceum Schott and white tannia grown from Xanthosoma sagittifolium (L.) Schott, of the Araceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Tannias for industrial processing are excluded.
sections.retrieval_text:
DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
number=1
level=1
This Standard applies to the tubercles of commercial varieties of lilac tannia grown from Xanthosoma violaceum Schott and white tannia grown from Xanthosoma sagittifolium (L.) Schott, of the Araceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Tannias for industrial processing are excluded.

page_spans.text: This Standard applies to the tubercles of commercial varieties of lilac tannia grown from Xanthosoma violaceum Schott and white tannia grown from Xanthosoma sagittifolium (L.) Schott, of the Araceae family, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging. Tannias for industrial processing are excluded.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY
sections.retrieval_text:
PROVISIONS CONCERNING QUALITY
number=2
level=1

sections.title: Minimum Requirements
sections.text: In all classes, subject to the special provisions for each class and the tolerances allowed, the tannias must be: whole; sound, produce affected by rotting or deterioration such as to make it unfit for consumption is excluded; clean, practically free of any visible foreign matter, except permitted substances used to prolong their shelf life; practically free of pests affecting the general appearance of the produce; practically free of damage caused by pests; free of abnormal external moisture, excluding condensation following removal from cold storage; free of any foreign smell and/or taste2; firm; practically free of mechanical damage and bruising; practically free of signs of sprouting.

sections.title: Classification
sections.text: Tannias are classified in three classes defined below:

sections.title: The tannias must have reached an appropriate degree of physiological development, account being taken of the characteristics of the variety and/or commercial type and the area in which they are grown.
path_titles: The tannias must have reached an appropriate degree of physiological development, account being taken of the characteristics of the variety and/or commercial type and the area in which they are grown.
sections.text: The development and condition of the tannias must be such as to enable them: to withstand transport and handling; and to arrive in satisfactory condition at the place of destination.

sections.title: “Extra” Class
sections.text: Tannias in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

page_spans.text: Tannias in this class must be of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

sections.title: Class I
sections.text: Tannias in this class must be of good quality. They must be characteristic of the variety. The following slight defects, however, may be allowed, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package: slight defects in shape; scarring, provided this does not cover more than 20% of the surface area; scraped areas, provided these do not exceed 20% of the surface area. The defects must not, in any case, affect the flesh of the produce.

page_spans.text: Tannias in this class must be of good quality. They must be characteristic of the variety. The following slight defects, however, may be allowed, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package: slight defects in shape; scarring, provided this does not cover more than 20% of the surface area; scraped areas, provided these do not exceed 20% of the surface area. The defects must not, in any case, affect the flesh of the produce.

sections.title: Class II
sections.text: This class includes tannias which do not qualify for inclusion in the higher classes, but satisfy the minimum requirements specified in Section 2.1 above. The following defects, however , may be allowed, provided the tannias retain their essential characteristics as regards the quality, the keeping quality and presentation: defects in shape; scarring, provided this does not cover more than 30% of the surface area; scraped areas, provided these do not exceed 30% of the surface area.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING
sections.text: Size is determined by the weight of the tannia, in accordance with the following table: Size Code Weight (in grams) Length Diameter A 150 - 249 Between 100 and 300 mm (measured at the convex part of root) 45 to 70 mm (measured at the widest cross section) B 250 - 349 C 350 - 450

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES
sections.text: Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.

sections.title: Size Tolerances
sections.text: For all classes, 10% by number or weight of tannias corresponding to the size immediately above and/or below that indicated on the package.

sections.title: Uniformity
sections.text: The contents of each package must be uniform and contain only tannias of the same origin, variety and/or commercial type, quality and size. The visible part of the contents of the package must be representative of the entire contents.

sections.title: Packaging
sections.text: Tannias must be packed in such a way as to protect the produce properly. The materials used inside the package must be new3, clean, and of a quality such as to avoid causing any external or internal damage to the produce. The use of materials, particularly of paper or stamps bearing trade specifications is allowed, provided the printing or labelling has been done with non-toxic ink or glue. Tannias shall be packed in each container in compliance with the Code of Practice for Packaging and Transport of Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 44-1995).

sections.title: Description of Containers
sections.text: The containers shall meet the quality, hygiene, ventilation and resistance characteristics to ensure suitable handling, shipping and preserving of the tannias. Packages must be free of all foreign matter and smell.

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text: 7.1 The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995). 7.2 The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

CONTAMINANTS
number=7
level=1

page_spans.text: 7.1 The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995). 7.2 The produce covered by this Standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text: 8.1 It is recommended that the produce covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CXC 53-2003), and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice. 8.2 The produce should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

pages.title: STANDARD FOR TANNIA1
pages.summary: Página contém título principal do documento e texto introdutório. Não há seções numeradas ou subtítulos evidentes.
Página contém seções numeradas de nível 1, 2 e 3. O texto está bem estruturado em torno dos títulos. A seção 2.2 "Classification" continua para próxima página.

A página 3 do documento apresenta seções numeradas estruturadas em níveis hierárquicos 1, 2 e 3. Titulações como '3. PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING', '4. PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES' e '5. PROVISIONS CONCERNING PRESENTATION' delineiam os níveis 1 principais. A seção 4 possui subseções claras como '4.1 Quality Tolerances' e subdivisões de nível 3. A seção 5 expande-se em subseções e subsubseções, com textos associados corretamente. O cabeçalho da página está identificado ('CXS 224-2001 3'). Não há indicações de continuidade entre páginas nesta página.

A página apresenta o início da seção 6 com duas subseções principais (6.1 e 6.2) subdivididas em vários subsubtítulos, além das seções 7 e 8 inteiramente nesta página. Cada título estruturado tem seu texto adequado associado, sem sinais de continuidade entre páginas.
